preview_text:
The model rates Colombia as a heavy favorite, assigning an 86% win probability and relegating Ghana to a near-zero chance of victory. The underdog is given only a 14% probability in the draw column, implying the model expects a decisive Colombian result.

The W/D/L spread reflects a wide gap: the model sees Colombia as substantially more likely to win than either draw or Ghana victory. A draw remains the only plausible alternative to a Colombian win in the model's forecast; any Ghana upset falls outside the statistical margin. The match is not expected to be closely contested by these metrics.

The market consensus diverges sharply from the model. Sportsbooks assign Colombia 63% (23 percentage points lower than the model), while placing Ghana's win probability at 13% and a draw at 24%. Polymarket sentiment aligns closely with the books. The dashboard does not adjudicate which source better reflects match reality; the divergence is material and warrants attention.
